title: What ET Prime is
text: ET Prime is presented as a members-only business storytelling platform from The Economic Times. It is best described as a premium membership for deeper business analysis rather than just a paywall for isolated articles.

title: Core ET Prime benefits
text: The ET Prime FAQ says members get insights across 20+ industries, ePaper access, ET market tools, Wealth edition, a cleaner experience with minimal ads, community engagement, and invites to virtual events.

title: Membership access rules
text: ET Prime membership is described as cross-platform. The FAQ says members do not buy separate desktop-only or app-only access; one membership works on all platforms.

title: Trial status conflict
text: Public ET pages show conflicting signals on ET Prime trials. The FAQ says no trial is currently offered, while other public ET surfaces have shown free-trial language. Answers about trials should be conflict-aware and ask users to verify the latest checkout screen.

title: Value surfaces on plans page
text: The ET Prime plans page highlights comprehensive stock reports, cleaner reading with fewer ads, Wealth Edition, ET Print Edition, and partner benefits as part of the membership value story.

title: ET Markets app overview
text: The ET Markets app is positioned as a stock tools and news app for both beginner and experienced investors, combining real-time market updates, research stories, watchlists, and actionable market tools.

title: Advanced market tools
text: The Google Play listing says ET Markets includes 4000+ stock reports, Big Bull Portfolio, Market Mood, Market Pulse, stock screeners, smart alerts, live streams, and stock recommendations.

title: Prime link inside ET Markets
text: The ET Markets app listing explicitly links premium benefits to ET Prime, saying ET Prime unlocks market research stories, advanced market tools, ePaper, and related benefits inside the broader ET ecosystem.

title: How to explain Market Mood
text: Market Mood should be explained as an ET tool that gives an overall view of market state using simplified visual signals, helping users quickly understand whether the market mood is bullish, cautious, or weak.

title: How to explain Stock Screener
text: ET's stock screener should be positioned as a discovery tool for filtering stocks using many data points, with AI-mode creation cues on the official screener surface.

title: How to explain Stock Reports Plus
text: Stock Reports Plus is ET's deeper stock-analysis benefit. It is useful when the user wants ratings, forecasts, peer comparison, or a structured explanation of company fundamentals and momentum.

title: What ET Portfolio is
text: ET Portfolio is a portfolio and goals surface for investors who want a single window to monitor holdings, watchlists, alerts, and goals, with mobile-friendly language around real-time updates and SIP support.

title: What Wealth Edition offers
text: Wealth Edition is framed as a weekly money management guide with investment planning ideas plus mutual fund and stock research. It is publicly positioned as a benefit available with ET Prime membership.

title: Print Edition benefit
text: ET Print Edition should be described as the digital newspaper/edition-style reading benefit that ET Prime members can access online.

title: What ETMasterclass is
text: ETMasterclass is ET's executive learning platform spanning Guru Masterclasses, Executive Workshops, and Certification Courses. It is relevant for users asking about professional upskilling, AI learning, management, or enterprise programs.

title: Who ETMasterclass is for
text: ETMasterclass describes itself as designed for working professionals, with flexible learning, industry-acknowledged certificates, peer cohort learning, and instruction from industry leaders.

title: Scale signals on ETMasterclass
text: The public ETMasterclass homepage shows scale signals including 200+ trainings, 6000+ executives, and 350+ experts. These numbers are useful for product positioning but should still be treated as page-level claims.

title: What ET Events means in the ecosystem
text: ET Events is best treated as a network of ET-owned event portals rather than one single product page. The portals let the concierge guide users toward live summits, enterprise events, and community touchpoints across multiple verticals.

title: How to use event detail pages
text: Event detail pages such as summit pages are useful for richer RAG answers about dates, themes, and registration, but should be treated as secondary pages beneath the portal-level event registry.

title: How to describe ET complimentary benefits
text: The ET benefits portal shows that ET Prime value extends into complimentary subscriptions and partner offers such as Times Prime, TOI+, Docubay, Sony LIV via OTTplay, Amazon Prime, and other redemption-style offers.

title: Important benefit activation constraint
text: The benefits portal says TimesPrime activation is limited to an Indian mobile number. Concierge answers about benefit activation should ask a quick eligibility question before making a promise.

title: Suggested beginner routing logic
text: If a user is new to ET and wants a place to start, the safest sequence is: explain ET Prime as the broad membership entry point, offer ET Markets for market-tool usage, offer Wealth Edition for personal finance reading, and offer ETMasterclass if the user wants structured learning.

title: Suggested learning routing logic
text: If a user asks for learning, certification, upskilling, or executive programs, route first to ETMasterclass rather than ET Prime or ET Markets, then narrow by topic such as AI, management, finance, or HR.

title: Suggested investor routing logic
text: If a user wants market tracking, stock tools, watchlists, or screeners, ET Markets and ET Portfolio are the strongest first destinations; ET Prime should be presented as the premium layer that deepens these workflows.

title: Ownership/legal anchor
text: ET platform pages and terms are useful as legal anchors for site ownership and for checking offer-related contradictions. They should support trust and verification logic rather than being overused in user-facing retrieval.
